caption: The video appears to be a screen recording of an IRS webpage discussing 2020 coronavirus-related distributions for retirement accounts. The page explains that since Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs) were waived in 2020, account owners or beneficiaries who received an RMD had the option to return it to their IRA or other qualified plan to avoid paying taxes on the distribution. It further details that a 2020 RMD qualifying as a coronavirus-related distribution can be repaid over a three-year period or have the taxes due on the distribution spread over three years. Additionally, it notes that a 2020 withdrawal from an inherited IRA cannot be repaid but may be spread over three years for income inclusion. The video likely serves as an informational guide for individuals navigating tax implications related to retirement accounts during the pandemic.
